Home.title: YieldField
Home.subtitle: AI-powered market finance — a daily briefing by a virtual chartist.

Hero.tagline: The market speaks. The Chartiste interprets.
Hero.disclaimer: The information presented is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice.

Common.disclaimer: This is not investment advice. Editorial content generated by AI for informational and demonstrative purposes.

Coulisses.title: Behind YieldField
Coulisses.subtitle: How a virtual chartist was born from a method, a pipeline, and an obsession with clarity.

step1.title: The Original Idea
step1.description: It all started with a simple question: why don't ordinary people have access to a real daily financial briefing? Bryan, a Product Owner passionate about markets, wanted to create an automated financial magazine — beautiful, accurate, accessible — without the barriers of professional tools at €200/month.
step1.quote: I wanted my grandmother to understand what's happening in the markets this morning.

step2.title: The BMAD Method
step2.description: To structure the project, we adopted BMAD v6.3.0 — an AI agile methodology that transforms a product vision into implementable stories. From Analyst to PM, from Architect to Dev: each agent has its role, each story has its acceptance criteria. 6 weeks to deliver 35 stories, 93 points, 224 tests.
step2.diagramAlt: BMAD Workflow: Analyst → PM → Architect → Dev → Review

step3.title: The Nightly Pipeline
step3.description: Every night at 2am UTC, GitHub Actions orchestrates the magic: data collection (Finnhub, FRED, Alpha Vantage), VIX percentile calculation, Claude Sonnet call to generate the bilingual briefing, publication to Cloudflare R2. By morning, the data is fresh and ready.
step3.diagramAlt: Pipeline: GitHub Actions → APIs → Claude → R2 → Site

step4.title: From v01 to v06: The Art of Prompting
step4.description: Six iterations to transform a generic prompt into a virtual chartist with a real personality. Each version brought more structure, sector context, and editorial nuance. Here is the complete evolution.

step5.title: Cloudflare Deployment
step5.description: Next.js 15 on Cloudflare Pages — €8/month all-in. 166 kB bundle, SSR/ISR rendering, data revalidated every hour. 224 tests, 4 security gates, zero performance compromise.

v01.label: v01
v01.notes: First draft — too generic, no personality

v03.label: v03
v03.notes: Added JSON structure + VIX alert level

v04.label: v04
v04.notes: Added editorial tone + theme of the day

v06.label: v06
v06.notes: Production version — full personality + strict bilingual output
